signal_title: 電子商取引及び情報財取引等に関する準則の改訂
risk_rationale: The regulatory signal from Japan involves updates to e-commerce guidelines, including copyright law amendments and blockchain technology use cases. These changes may impact TikTok's e-commerce and content moderation policies, but do not pose an immediate existential threat.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Reflect blockchain technology use cases in value transfer disclosures
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not currently account for blockchain technology in live commerce disclosures.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Misinformation during elections: Prebunking partnerships, fact-check labels
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Reflect changes in copyright law in content moderation practices
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not explicitly address copyright law changes in content moderation.

remediations.title: Update Live Commerce Disclosures for Blockchain
remediations.description: Integrate blockchain technology use cases into live commerce disclosure mechanisms to ensure compliance with new Japanese guidelines.
acceptance_criteria: Blockchain use cases are disclosed in live commerce

remediations.title: Revise Content Moderation for Copyright Compliance
remediations.description: Update content moderation practices to reflect changes in Japanese copyright law, ensuring that content is reviewed and moderated in accordance with new legal standards.
acceptance_criteria: Content moderation reflects updated copyright law
No copyright infringement issues in Japan

business_impact: The updates may require adjustments in live commerce and content moderation practices, potentially affecting user engagement and compliance costs in Japan.
recommended_actions: Review and integrate blockchain technology disclosures in live commerce
Update content moderation practices for copyright compliance
Monitor further regulatory developments in Japan
